note: Headless Godot runner not applicable for web-demo TypeScript build. Report produced via static analysis of all 16 minigame files + HUD.ts + OghamPanel.ts + main.ts.

files_verified: mg_combat_rituel.ts: private ended=false (L29), reset in setup() (L117), guard in endGame() (L192-193)
mg_regard.ts: private ended=false (L42), reset in setup() (L115), guard in endGame() (L229-230)
mg_course.ts: private ended=false (L42), reset in setup() (L129), guard in endGame() (L236-237)
mg_fouille.ts: private ended=false (L50), reset in setup() (L138), guard in endGame() (L235-236)
mg_herboristerie.ts: private ended=false (L70), reset in setup() (L139), guard in endGame() (L266-267)
c78_ended_guard_6_minigames.note: mg_equilibre was listed in C78 scope but grep confirms no ended field — see NEW BUGS below.

evidence: HUD.ts L30: module-level let _hudUnsubscribe: (() => void) | null = null
Placement: module scope (not inside function body) — correct

mg_apaisement.race_risk: LOW — single trigger path, no concurrent user action path to endGame

mg_negociation.race_risk: LOW — pointer handler accumulates score, does not call endGame

mg_ombres.race_risk: LOW — pointermove does not call endGame

mg_sang_froid.race_risk: LOW — pointermove/pointerdown do not call endGame

ogham_panel_subscriber_audit.evidence: OghamPanel.ts uses DOM addEventListener (not store.subscribe). initOghamPanel() has idempotency guard at L18: if (!app || document.getElementById('ogham-panel-overlay')) return. No subscriber accumulation possible.

direct_finish_bypass_audit.evidence: All 15 this.finish() calls across all minigames are located inside their respective endGame() methods. No call site bypasses endGame().

issues.issue: C78 claimed mg_equilibre received ended guard but grep finds no 'ended' field or guard. The file has endGame() called from both timer (via render loop) and potentially button action — needs confirmation.
issues.recommendation: Add private ended = false + setup() reset + endGame() guard to mg_equilibre to match the pattern applied to the other 5 C78 files.

issues.issue: 5 minigames lack the ended idempotency guard. Current risk is LOW (single timer-only trigger path per file), but the pattern is inconsistent across the codebase. Any future addition of a user-action shortcut to these games would immediately introduce a race.
issues.recommendation: Apply the same private ended = false pattern to all 5 for uniformity and future-safety.
